item.title: cmd_backlog_populate silently ignores unknown options
item.intent: cmd_backlog_populate must reject unknown flags — silently discarding options is inconsistent with the established error-handling pattern and hides user mistakes
item.description: cmd_backlog_populate() (around line 3457) performs no option parsing at all. Any flag passed to 'backlog populate' is silently ignored. Most other commands use a while/case loop with a catch-all that calls log_error and returns 1. Fix: add a proper option parsing loop. populate currently takes no flags, so any argument is an error — the loop body should be just the catch-all that rejects everything.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_populate() and identify the current argument handling (none)
Add a while/case loop before the function body that rejects unknown options with log_error 'Unknown option: $1' and returns 1
Verify that 'backlog populate' with no arguments still works correctly
Verify that 'backlog populate --typo' now prints an error and exits non-zero
acceptanceCriteria: backlog populate --unknown-flag exits with a non-zero status and prints 'Unknown option: --unknown-flag'
backlog populate with no arguments still works correctly
Error message matches the format used by other commands
